summary: Well-structured test suite covering all major requirements: parsing (requirements.txt, package.json), license classification, report generation, CLI integration, and end-to-end GitHub Actions validation via act. TDD comments show good methodology. Good use of fixtures and helper functions (_run_cli, act harness). Gaps: missing tests for empty manifest files, empty dependency lists, missing/invalid config files (though error handling exists in implementation), empty config sections. Error scenario testing covers main paths but not all branches (_load_config error handling untested at unit level). Act test output parsing via regex markers is somewhat brittle and couples tests to specific workflow output format. Only 3 end-to-end act test cases—more edge case coverage would strengthen confidence. Unit tests are solid and would catch most logic bugs; act tests validate integration but are limited in scope.
